propositions: The long-running auto-lander loop service must be containerized and deployed as a single instance via docker-compose on the existing Gerrit box.
The service must log to its own service logs and never write to session_log.
All failure modes (hang, error, stuck-state, lander_down) must be loudly visible via metrics and alarms.
A heartbeat timestamp file written once per 15s poll drives a read-only HTTP status endpoint exposing heartbeat_age_s, with lander_down triggered at age > 90s.
A stuck-state time-in-phase alarm fires when a wipChain remains in a non-idle phase for more than 45 minutes.
The service requires crash-safe restart with single-writer flock, autoheal sidecar restart, drain-on-SIGTERM recovery record, and provisioned Gerrit credential plus Rebase capability.
